problem.title: Meeting Rooms II
problem.description: Given an array of meeting time intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of conference rooms required.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting
solutions.description: Sort intervals by start time and check whether any two consecutive meetings overlap. If a meeting starts before the previous one ends, a person cannot attend both, so the function returns false. The key insight is that sorting brings potentially conflicting meetings adjacent to each other, so a single linear scan suffices to detect any overlap.
